description_long: This object is a multi-tiered pagoda structure featuring three distinct roof levels, each with wide, gently sloping eaves. The roofs are finished in a dark gray, tile-like pattern, while the railings and trim are highlighted in a gold color, giving the building a striking contrast. The base of the pagoda is constructed with a brown, wood-like material, and the main body is white, suggesting traditional construction methods. A prominent ornamental finial sits atop the highest roof, adding a decorative flourish typical of East Asian pagodas. One side of the structure includes an attached annex with a matching roof, indicating a possible entrance or auxiliary space. The overall form and details evoke the style of Japanese pagodas, often found in temple complexes or gardens.
description: A three-tiered Japanese-style pagoda with dark gray tiled roofs, gold railings, and a decorative finial, featuring a side annex and traditional architectural details.
description_view_0: This view shows the pagoda from the side, highlighting the gold railings, the layered roofs, and the attached annex on the left.
description_view_1: The front view displays the central entrance area, the symmetrical roof tiers, and the prominent gold finial at the top.
description_view_2: This side view mirrors view 0 but from the opposite side, showing the annex on the right and the layered roof structure.
description_view_3: The rear view reveals the back of the pagoda, with the gold railings and the brown base clearly visible, and no annex attached.
